caption: Rama and Sugriva vow friendship around the sacred fire
scene: Rama and Sugriva circle the sacred fire (agni) together, taking vows of alliance and friendship, with Lakshmana and Hanuman witnessing. The sealing of the bond that leads to the search for Sita.
tool: PLACEHOLDER — record the actual generator when the real image is added
prompt: Traditional Tanjore (Thanjavur) style devotional painting, wide landscape 16:9: Lord Rama and the vanara king Sugriva circling a sacred fire together to take vows of alliance, with Lakshmana and Hanuman witnessing. Rama and Lakshmana are forest ascetics — matted hair (jatamukuta), bark-fiber garments (valkala), no crowns or royal jewelry; Rama holds his tall kodanda longbow (as tall as himself). Rama blue-skinned with a halo. Rich jewel-tone colors, gold-leaf gilded ornamentation and halos, ornate arched temple border, serene rounded figures, forest setting, classical South Indian temple art, no text, no lettering.
